task_completion.description: A publish profile is created or opened in Visual Studio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio 'Publish' tab is visible for the CalibrationChecker project, showing the publish profile overview.

task_completion.description: The target publish location is set to C:\PublishOutput.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Visual Studio publish profile settings or folder path configuration, the Target location is explicitly set to 'C:\PublishOutput'.

task_completion.description: The deployment mode is configured as Self-contained.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Visual Studio Profile settings dialog, the 'Deployment mode' dropdown is set to 'Self-contained'.

task_completion.description: The target runtime is configured as win-x64.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Visual Studio Profile settings dialog, the 'Target runtime' dropdown is set to 'win-x64'.

task_completion.description: The 'Produce single file' option is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Visual Studio Profile settings dialog under File publish options, the checkbox for 'Produce single file' is checked.
Visual Studio displays a 'Publish succeeded' message in the publish tab or the Output window shows a successful build and publish process.

task_completion.description: The final standalone executable is present in the target directory with no accompanying .dll files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File Explorer is open to 'C:\PublishOutput' showing 'CalibrationChecker.exe' with a large file size (at least 40,000 KB / 40 MB). There are no .dll files visible in this folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the Visual Studio GUI to configure and publish the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Visual Studio's Publish dialogs and settings windows, rather than just typing 'dotnet publish' commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual CalibrationChecker project.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual Studio shows the 'CalibrationChecker' project loaded in the Solution Explorer and the publish profile is clearly tied to this project.

integrity.description: The output executable appears to be a genuine self-contained .NET application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file size of CalibrationChecker.exe in File Explorer is substantial (around 40MB or larger), indicating it actually contains the bundled .NET runtime, rather than being a tiny dummy file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target publish directory: C:\PublishOutput. Expected executable name: CalibrationChecker.exe. Minimum expected file size for the self-contained executable: 40 MB. Expected target runtime: win-x64. The final output folder must not contain any .dll files.
